results.comment_text:
@user1:
```suggestion
        "d": pandas.to_datetime(
            [
                "2018-10-26 12:00",
                "2018-10-26 13:00:15",
                "2020-10-26 04:00:15",
                "2020-10-26",
            ]
        ),
```
the "d" column has only 2 elements when others have 4. This makes the test fail silently at the DataFrames construction stage: [this piece of code](https://github.com/modin-project/modin/blob/0f5a47177025b069e74de9d8caff17e1e6b24fec/modin/experimental/core/execution/native/implementations/omnisci_on_native/test/utils.py#L261-L278) tries to build modin and pandas dataframes from the given data and considers that the test passes if they both raise an error (always happen if the data is incorrect).

I think we have to add a parameter or smth else in a separate PR to explicitly turn this behavior on, so no implicit silent errors occur. 

@author:
Yeah, my bad...
results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_datetime_data_column_d_has_four_elements_not_two__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/modin/experimental/core/execution/native/implementations/omnisci_on_native/test/test_dataframe.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    assert 'def test_dt_hour' in source, "Expected 'test_dt_hour' to exist in TestDateTime"

def test_datetime_data_column_d_has_four_elements_not_two__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/modin/experimental/core/execution/native/implementations/omnisci_on_native/test/test_dataframe.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('"d"\\s*:\\s*pandas\\.to_datetime\\(\\s*\\[(?P<items>.*?)\\]\\s*\\)', source, flags=re.S)
    assert m is not None, 'Expected TestDateTime.datetime_data to define column "d" using pandas.to_datetime([...])'

def test_datetime_data_column_d_has_four_elements_not_two__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/modin/experimental/core/execution/native/implementations/omnisci_on_native/test/test_dataframe.py', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    m = re.search('"d"\\s*:\\s*pandas\\.to_datetime\\(\\s*\\[(?P<items>.*?)\\]\\s*\\)', source, flags=re.S)
    items = m.group('items')
    n_items = len(re.findall('"\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}[^"]*"', items))
    assert n_items == 4, f'TestDateTime.datetime_data["d"] must have 4 datetime values to match other columns. Found {n_items}. Having only 2 values can make run_and_compare pass silently due to both pandas and modin failing at DataFrame construction.'
